turns.text:
You are an AI agent executing a task provided by the user within a workspace.

## Workspace layout

Everything you work with lives under one workspace root. **`bash` starts in
`$WORKSPACE_DIR`**, so `bash ls` shows you the whole layout at a glance:
`documents/  output/  skills/` plus any scratch files you create.

- **`$WORKSPACE_DIR`** — your working area, default `bash` cwd. Use it for
  notes, intermediate files, and skill output. Skill scripts live at
  `$WORKSPACE_DIR/skills/<name>/scripts/`.
- **`$DOCUMENTS_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/documents`) — task documents.
  Read-only.
- **`$OUTPUT_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/output`) — deliverables. The harness
  routes relative `write` and `edit` paths here automatically.
- **Task configuration** (`task.json`) — contains the task definition and the
  grading rubric. Do not read, search, or reference it. Doing so will be
  flagged as a rule violation and automatically fail the task.

## Tool conventions

- Use `read` to consume input files (handles .docx, .xlsx, .pptx, .pdf, and
  plain text).
- Use the file-type skill manuals below to produce binary deliverables
  (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx).
- Use `write` only for plain markdown — typically a `response.md`
  summarizing your work.
- Use `edit` for incremental refinement of a file you have already created.

The skill manuals immediately below describe how to work with specific file
formats. Read them before tackling the task.


## Skill: docx

For READING existing .docx files, use the harness `re

Hi Rachel,

I wanted to reach out regarding staffing on the MedCore discovery work.

As you may be aware, we received MedCore's third document production late las=
t week =E2=80=94 approximately 85,000 documents. With depositions of former P=
innacle employees now being scheduled for May, including Dr. Rebecca Liu, and=
 the September 30 discovery close deadline approaching, we need to make sure =
we're staying on track. James Pettersson has been doing excellent work handli=
ng interrogatory responses and supervising the document review consistent wit=
h the staffing plan approved back in February, but the volume from this lates=
t production is straining his capacity.

To that end, we'd like to bring on Nina Barros, another mid-level associate h=
ere at the firm, to assist James through the remainder of the discovery phase=
. Nina is a fourth-year associate and would bill at the same $475/hr mid-leve=
l rate as James. Her role would include assisting with interrogatory response=
s, conducting research on the trade secret preemption issues we've been track=
ing, and reviewing deposition transcripts as they become available. Nina has =
solid experience in commercial litigation and has worked on several matters i=
nvolving complex, high-volume document productions. She currently has availab=
ility to take this on. We believe adding her is necessary to meet the discove=
ry deadlines without sacrificing the quality of our work product.

In terms of hours, we'd estimate Nina would bill approximately 40=E2=80=9350 =
hours per month through the end of the discovery phase. That will increase th=
e monthly attorney fees somewhat, but the team believes this is the most effi=
cient approach given the volume we're dealing with rather than trying to stre=
tch James too thin.

Please let us know if you'd like to discuss further or if you need any additi=
onal information about Nina's background. Happy to set up a call if that's ea=
sier.

Karen

February 10, 2025

Rachel Dominguez, Esq. Associate General Counsel Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. 2800 Peachtree Road NE, Suite 1400 Atlanta, Georgia 30305

> **Re:** *Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. v. MedCore Analytics, LLC*, Case No. 1:24-cv-04187-RWS (N.D. Ga.) --- Proposed Discovery-Phase Staffing Plan

**cc:** Sandra Whitmore, General Counsel, Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc.

Dear Ms. Dominguez:

Thank you for the continued confidence that Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. (\"Pinnacle\") has placed in Hargrove & Linden LLP (the \"Firm\") in connection with the above-captioned matter. As you know, Pinnacle engaged the Firm in September 2024 to prosecute claims against MedCore Analytics, LLC (\"MedCore\") arising from the departure of three former Pinnacle data scientists and the alleged misappropriation of Pinnacle\'s proprietary predictive-analytics algorithms and related confidential information. The claims asserted by Pinnacle include breach of contract (specifically, violations of non-disclosure and non-competition agreements), misappropriation of trade secrets under both the Georgia Trade Secrets Act of 1990 (O.C.G.A. § 10-1-760 *et seq.*) and the federal Defend Trade Secrets Act of 2016 (18 U.S.C. § 1836 *et seq.*), and unjust enrichment. MedCore, represented by Sternfield Morrison & Katz LLP, has asserted counterclaims for tortious interference with business relations and a declaratory judgment of non-infringement. The amount in controversy is approximately \$47.5 million in claimed damages, and the matter is presided over by the Honorable Richard W. Stanton.

Discovery in this matter commenced on February 3, 2025, pursuant to the Court\'s Scheduling Order, and the discovery close deadline is September 30, 2025. Given the scope and complexity of the anticipated discovery --- which will include voluminous document productions relating to proprietary algorithm source code and internal communications, depositions of the three former Pinnacle data scientists now employed by MedCore, extensive written interrogatories and requests for production, and likely contested motions to compel --- the Firm believes it is prudent and necessary to submit a comprehensive staffing plan for the discovery phase (February through September 2025).

This letter is submitted in accordance with Pinnacle\'s Outside Counsel Billing Guidelines (January 2023 version) (the \"Billing Guidelines\"), specifically **Section 4.2**, which requires that outside counsel obtain pre-approval of all timekeepers assigned to a matter and that any subsequent changes to the staffing plan receive written pre-approval from Pinnacle\'s designated in-house counsel. The timekeepers identified in Section 2 below constitute the **complete and exclusive** roster of individuals authorized to bill time on this matter during the discovery phase. **Only the timekeepers listed below are authorized to perform work on and bill for services in connection with this matter. Any additions to or substitutions within this roster require prior written approval from Pinnacle\'s designated in-house counsel in accordance with Section 4.2 of the Billing Guidelines.**

**[PINNACLE HEALTH SYSTEMS, INC.]{.underline}**

**[OUTSIDE COUNSEL BILLING GUIDELINES]{.underline}**

**Effective Date: January 1, 2023**

Issued by the Office of the General Counsel

Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. 2800 Peachtree Road NE, Suite 1400 Atlanta, GA 30305

These Guidelines are binding on all outside counsel engaged by Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. and its subsidiaries (collectively, \"Pinnacle\" or the \"Company\"). Acceptance of any engagement from Pinnacle --- whether by execution of an engagement letter, submission of an invoice, commencement of billable work, or any other action constituting performance of legal services on behalf of the Company --- constitutes agreement to comply with these Guidelines in their entirety. Outside Counsel is expected to familiarize all timekeepers assigned to Pinnacle matters with the contents of these Guidelines and to ensure full compliance at every level of the engagement team. Failure to comply with these Guidelines may result in reductions, rejections, or other remedies as set forth herein. Questions regarding the interpretation or application of these Guidelines should be directed to the Designated In-House Counsel for the applicable matter or to the Office of the General Counsel.

**[SECTION 1 --- PURPOSE AND SCOPE]{.underline}**

**1.1 Purpose.** These Guidelines establish the policies and procedures governing the retention, billing, and payment of outside counsel retained by Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. (\"Pinnacle\" or the \"Company\"), a Delaware corporation with principal offices in Atlanta, Georgia. The purpose of these Guidelines is to ensure cost-effective, transparent, and accountable legal representation across all matters handled by Outside Counsel on behalf of Pinnacle and its subsidiaries. Pinnacle operates fourteen (14) acute-care hospitals and affiliated physician practices across the southeastern United States, generating annual revenue of approximately \$2.8 billion. The Company maintains an internal legal department of twelve (12) attorneys under the leadership of the General Counsel. Pinnacle\'s litigation portfolio typically includes eight to twelve (8--12) active matters at any given time, with annual outside counsel expenditures of approximately \$6.5 million. Given the magnitude and recurring nature of this spend, disciplined cost management, predictable budgeting, and rigorous adherence to billing standards are essential to the Company\'s stewardship of corporate resources.

**1.2 Scope and Applicability**

These Guidelines apply to all law firms, attorneys, legal professionals, contract attorneys, staff attorneys, legal process outsourcing providers, and other legal service providers (collectively, \"Outside Counsel\") engaged by Pinnacle on any matter, whether litigation, regulatory, transactional, advisory, or otherwise. Outside Counsel\'s acceptance of an engagement letter, submission of an invoice, or performance of work on behalf of Pinnacle constitutes acceptance of and agreement to comply with these Guidelines in their entirety. In the event of a conflict between these Guidelines and an engagement letter or fee agreement, the engagement letter or fee agreement controls only to the extent the conflict is expressly identified therein and the deviation is set forth with specificity; in all other respects, these Guidelines govern. These Guidelines supersede all prior versions of Pinnacle\'s billing guidelines and any prior oral or written representations regarding billing practices. The General Counsel, currently Sandra Whitmore, or her designee has the authority to grant written exceptions to any provision of these Guidelines on a case-by-case basis.

**1.3 Amendments.** Pinnacle reserves the right to amend these Guidelines at any time. Amendments will be communicated in writing to all active Outside Counsel and will apply to all invoices submitted after the effective date of the amendment. Continued performance of work or submission of invoices following receipt of an amendment constitutes acceptance of the amended Guidelines.

**[SECTION 2 --- DEFINITIONS]{.underline}**

The following terms, when used in these Guidelines with initial capitalization, shall have the meanings set forth below:

**\"Administrative Task\"** means a task of a non-legal, clerical, or logistical nature, including but not limited to: scheduling meetings and depositions, coordinating with court reporters or vendors, arranging travel or accommodation, filing documents with courts through electronic filing systems (mechanical filing, not the drafting of the document being filed), managing calendar entries, updating service lists or distribution lists, and similar activities. Administrative Tasks are not compensable as attorney time and are considered part of Outside Counsel\'s overhead.

**\"Approved Staffing Plan\"** means the written staffing plan for a specific matter, approved by Pinnacle\'s Designated In-House Counsel, identifying each timekeeper by name, role, billing rate, and anticipated monthly hours, as further described in Section 4.1.

**\"Block Billing\"** means a time entry that combines two or more discrete tasks into a single line item without separately allocating time to each task. Block Billing is prohibited under Section 5.4.

**\"Contract Attorney\"** or **\"Staff Attorney\"** means a non-partner, non-associate attorney engaged on a temporary or project basis, including attorneys sourced through legal staffing vendors, legal process outsourcing providers, or the firm\'s own contract staffing program.

**\"Court Appearance\"** means an appearance before a judicial officer in a courtroom proceeding, including hearings, oral arguments, trials, and status conferences held in the courthouse. For the avoidance of doubt, depositions are not Court Appearances, regardless of whether a judge or magistrate is present by telephone for discovery dispute purposes.

**\"Designated In-House Counsel\"** means the Pinnacle attor

ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGED AND CONFIDENTIAL ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT --- PREPARED IN ANTICIPATION OF LITIGATION

**HARGROVE & LINDEN LLP** 195 Peachtree Street NE, Suite 3200 Atlanta, Georgia 30303 Telephone: (404) 555-7200 Facsimile: (404) 555-7201 www.hargrovelinden.com

June 1, 2025

**VIA EMAIL --- CONFIDENTIAL**

Rachel Dominguez, Esq. Associate General Counsel Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. 2800 Peachtree Road NE, Suite 1400 Atlanta, Georgia 30305

> **Re:** Monthly Case Status Report --- May 2025 Activity *Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. v. MedCore Analytics, LLC* Case No. 1:24-cv-04187-RWS U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Georgia Before the Honorable Richard W. Stanton
>
> **cc:** Sandra Whitmore, General Counsel, Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. David Hargrove, Lead Partner, Hargrove & Linden LLP

Dear Rachel:

This memorandum provides the monthly case status report for the above-referenced matter, covering activity during the reporting period of May 1 through May 31, 2025. Please note that this communication is protected by the attorney-client privilege and constitutes attorney work product prepared in anticipation of litigation. It should not be disclosed to any third party without prior consultation with this firm.

**[I. Executive Summary]{.underline}**

May 2025 was an exceptionally active month in the *Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. v. MedCore Analytics, LLC* litigation. Three major workstreams dominated the team\'s efforts during the reporting period: (1) receipt and initial review of MedCore\'s third document production, which comprised approximately 85,000 documents and constituted the largest single production in the case to date; (2) the deposition of Dr. Rebecca Liu, one of the three former Pinnacle data scientists at the center of this dispute, which took place on May 19, 2025; and (3) the filing of a motion to compel before Judge Stanton regarding MedCore\'s incomplete and evasive responses to Pinnacle\'s First Set of Interrogatories.

The convergence of these critical workstreams required the litigation team to operate at elevated capacity throughout the month. Mr. Hargrove\'s involvement was significantly more extensive than in prior months due to the critical nature of the Liu deposition, the strategy session with Pinnacle\'s in-house team, and his personal review of the third document production. Specifically, Mr. Hargrove devoted substantial personal attention to the matter this month, including attending the Liu deposition, conducting a comprehensive strategy session with the in-house team, and personally reviewing key documents in the third production. The Liu deposition was staffed to ensure comprehensive coverage given its critical importance to Pinnacle\'s trade secret claims, as Dr. Liu possesses direct knowledge of the proprietary algorithms at the heart of this case.

Looking ahead, the discovery close deadline remains September 30, 2025, leaving the team approximately four months to complete fact depositions, pursue any supplemental discovery compelled by the court, and initiate expert discovery. The sections below provide a detailed account of each workstream and the outlook for June and July.

**[II. Case Background and Current Posture]{.underline}**

For the monthly record, the following summarizes the current posture of the litigation. This action was filed by Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. against MedCore Analytics, LLC, a Georgia limited liability company headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, on September 15, 2024. Pinnacle\'s claims include breach of contract arising from non-disclosure and non-competition agreements, misappropriation of trade secrets under both the Georgia Trade Secrets Act and the federal Defend Trade Secrets Act (\"DTSA\"), 18 U.S.C. § 1836 *et seq.*, and unjust enrichment. The amount in controversy, as stated in Pinnacle\'s complaint, is approximately \$47.5 million in claimed damages.

MedCore, represented by Sternfield Morrison & Katz LLP, has asserted counterclaims against Pinnacle for tortious interference with business relations and seeks a declaratory judgment of non-infringement of any protectable trade secrets.

The core factual allegations center on MedCore\'s hiring of three former Pinnacle data scientists---including Dr. Rebecca Liu---and MedCore\'s alleged use of Pinnacle\'s proprietary patient-outcome prediction algorithms to develop competing analytics products. Pinnacle alleges that the departing employees transported proprietary data and source code to MedCore in violation of their contractual obligations.

Discovery commenced on February 3, 2025, pursuant to the scheduling order entered by Judge Stanton. The discovery close deadline is September 30, 2025. The case is currently in the fact discovery phase, with the team focused on document review, depositions of former Pinnacle employees now employed by MedCore, and interrogatory responses from both sides.

**[III. May 2025 Activity Report]{.underline}**

**[III.A. MedCore\'s Third Document Production]{.underline}**

In early May 2025, MedCore, through its counsel at Sternfield Morrison & Katz LLP, served its third document production in this matter. The production consisted of approximately 85,000 documents totaling over 1.2 million pages, making it by a significant margin the largest single production in the case to date. The prior two productions combined had totaled approximately 40,000 documents. The substantial increase in volume appears attributable to the production of internal MedCore email communications, technical specifications and design documents, source code documentation, and communications involving the three former Pinnacle data scientists, including extensive correspondence to and from Dr. Rebecca Liu regarding algorithm development at MedCore.

The production was hosted and processed by Tripoint Litigation Support LLC, the firm\'s e-discovery hosting vendor. Tripoint completed ingestion, de-duplication, and processing of the producti

tool_calls.name: bash
tool_calls.cmd:
cd $WORKSPACE_DIR && python3 -c "
import openpyxl
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook('documents/hl-may-2025-invoice.xlsx', data_only=True)
print('SHEETS:', wb.sheetnames)
print()
ws = wb['Time Detail']
print('Time Detail dims:', ws.dimensions, 'max_row:', ws.max_row, 'max_col:', ws.max_column)
print()
# Print header row
for r in range(1, 4):
    row=[]
    for c in range(1, ws.max_column+1):
        v = ws.cell(r,c).value
        row.append(str(v)[:25] if v is not None else '')
    print(r, '|', ' | '.join(row))
"

tool_calls.name: bash
tool_calls.cmd:
cd $WORKSPACE_DIR && python3 -c "
import openpyxl
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook('documents/hl-may-2025-invoice.xlsx', data_only=True)
ws = wb['Time Detail']
from collections import defaultdict
totals = defaultdict(lambda: [0.0,0.0])
entries=[]
for r in range(2, ws.max_row+1):
    entry = ws.cell(r,1).value
    if entry is None: 
        # could be subtotal row
        label = ws.cell(r,1).value
        if label and 'SUBTOTAL' in str(label):
            print('SUBTOTAL row:', [ws.cell(r,c).value for c in range(1,8)])
        continue
    date = ws.cell(r,2).value
    tk = ws.cell(r,3).value
    role = ws.cell(r,4).value
    rate = ws.cell(r,5).value
    hrs = ws.cell(r,6).value
    fee = ws.cell(r,7).value
    code = ws.cell(r,8).value
    desc = ws.cell(r,9).value
    if tk is None: 
        continue
    totals[tk][0]+=hrs
    totals[tk][1]+=fee
    entries.append((entry,date,tk,role,rate,hrs,fee,code,desc))
print('=== Per-timekeeper totals (computed) ===')
for tk,(h,f) in totals.items():
    print(f'{tk:20s} hrs={h:6.1f}  fee={f:11.2f}')
print('TOTAL hrs=', sum(v[0] for v in totals.values()), ' fee=', sum(v[1] for v in totals.values()))
print('Num entries:', len(entries))
"

---
"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

turns.text:
The deliverable is complete and validated. Here is a summary of what I produced.

## Deliverable: `output/invoice-review-memo.docx`

A detailed, schema-valid Word memorandum reviewing Hargrove & Linden LLP Invoice No. HL-2025-05-PIN against the Approved Staffing Plan, Pinnacle's Billing Guidelines, the staffing emails, and the May 2025 Case Status Report.

### Key findings and reduction calculations

**Critical defect — internal reconciliation (§9.1):** The 117 line items in the Time Detail total **$215,680.00** in fees, but the Summary page (and the detail's own SUBTOTAL row) claim only **$193,372.50** — a **$22,307.50** discrepancy. The per-timekeeper totals don't foot either. The invoice cannot be paid as submitted; a corrected, reconciled invoice is required.

**Attorney-fee reductions ($73,036.88):**

| Ref. | Issue | Guideline | Reduction |
|---|---|---|---|
| A | Unauthorized timekeepers — Nina Barros ($23,275) + Priya Sengupta ($10,125) | §4.2 | $33,400.00 |
| B | Contract attorney billed at $210 vs. approved $185 (and $200 cap) | §5.1, §4.4 | $3,862.50 |
| C | Excess timekeepers — May 8 call (5 TKs, cap 3) + May 19 deposition (3 TKs, cap 2) | §4.3 | $3,312.50 |
| D | Block billing — 52 entries × 25% | §5.4 | $29,864.38 |
| E | Partner cite-checking + junior-associate routine doc review | §6.1 | $1,885.00 |
| F | Administrative scheduling billed as attorney time | §6.1(g)–(h) | $712.50 |

**Expense reduction:** $2,100.00 travel disallowed (deposition was at H&L's own Atlanta office → local travel; meals not out-of-town; no >$1,000 pre-approval). Court-reporting expedited premium flagged pending an itemized breakdown.

**Budget (§7.3):** Fees as submitted exceed the $189,750 ceiling without pre-approval — a documented violation; the specific reductions bring compliant fees below the ceiling, so no additional cap reduction is needed. Missing budget-to-actual reconciliation (§7.2/§9.1(g)) also flagged.

### Bottom line

| | Amount |
|---|---|
| Compliant attorney fees | $142,643.12 |
| Compliant expenses | $6,350.00 |
| **Recommended total payable** | **$148,993.12** |
| Amount requested | $201,822.50 |
| **Total reduction** | **$52,829.38 (26.2%)** |

The memo includes an executive summary, document-by-document findings tied to specific guideline sections, a reduction-calculation summary, a per-timekeeper reconciliation, recommendations/next steps, and two appendices (a full 52-entry block-billing schedule and a fully-rejected/rate-reduced entry schedule).
